I've been coming here for years for various repairs and routine maintenance. But, based on my last experience, I think time for Mavis to remove the word "Discount" from their name.

Generally, the staff is nice and they get the job done promptly if you come during their off-peak hours.

However, managament seems to change every year, and the prices for service get dramatically higher with each iteration.

This was once my go-to spot for oil changes. I have a 4 cyclinder American made car and would bring my own preferred 5-liter synthetic oil and filter, then just pay for labor costs.

Around 2020-2021 they charged around $15-$20 for labor on an oil change. Then it increased to around $30. Recently, in 2025, they charged $95 just for labor or $115 with the oil and filter included!

Based on that quote, they now charge around $75-$100 for around 15 minutes of labor!

Also, beware of the bait and switch.

For example, during one visit for an oil change, I called ahead and got a price. When I arrived, I confirmed the price.

But, when it was time to pay, the manager gave me a higher price and said it was because the pricing codes were wrong. The price is the price!

I understand mechanics often want to markup prices and add-on services when possible, but it's not fair to the customer to change the price at the end. Especially not for standard maintenance tasks.

Finally, I had one bad experience here when a mechanic actually forgot to put oil in my engine for an oil change. I figured this out while driving away and my car alerted me to check my oil. So, be sure to check your repairs before you leave.

Honesty and honoring prices are big deals when it comes to service. And of course, businesses can increase prices. But increasing prices by over 5x during the course of a few years isn't going to keep customers.

Given the mixed experiences and rapidly increasing prices, I'm looking elsewhere for auto services. Mavis has officially priced me out.

Right now, Walmart Auto charges $59 for a full synthetic oil change. That's the baseline for my next oil change and dictates where I'll be doing any maintenance going forward - it won't be here.

However, I have not receive any communication from Mavis Discount Tire. I'm still waiting for Mavis to make this right. It has been almost two weeks since I have made the initial request to Mavis to remedy their negligent work.

Walking into Mavis Discount Tire in Jersey City the first time, I expected what most people expect: tires, a wait, maybe a quick conversation, and that’s it. What I didn’t expect was to repeatedly witness the level of patience, structure, and professionalism that this location operates with on a daily basis.

Every visit has shown me the same thing: this shop is run by people who actually care.

From the moment you step in, you can feel that this place is different. The front counter isn’t chaos. It’s communication. It’s explanations. It’s honesty. Junior stands out immediately not because he’s loud, but because he’s calm, confident, and knowledgeable. He doesn’t rush people. He doesn’t talk over them. He breaks things down so customers understand what their car needs, why it needs it, and what their options are. That alone separates him from a lot of places.

What really pushed me to write this review is what I’ve personally witnessed as a customer.

I’ve sat in that waiting area and watched people walk in already angry. I’ve seen customers speak over the staff, cut them off mid-sentence, walk out in the middle of explanations, and demand services without even wanting to hear pricing. I’ve watched people refuse to listen, yet expect miracles. The kind of behavior most people don’t realize workers deal with every single day.

And through all of it, Junior, the manager, and the entire team never break character.

No attitudes. No sarcasm. No shortcuts. Just calm voices, clear explanations, and professionalism. Even when customers are rude, impatient, or disrespectful, this team continues to do what many places don’t anymore: they try to educate instead of argue. They explain instead of dismiss. They protect transparency instead of chasing quick money.

This shop does not believe in doing work without approval. They don’t believe in hiding prices. They don’t believe in rushing people into decisions. They believe in doing things the right way and you can feel that from management all the way down to the techs in the back.

Every time I’ve had my car serviced here, it’s been handled properly. The work is solid. The communication is clear. The environment is professional. And the respect between staff members shows you that this is a well run location, not just a place with a sign on the building.

In a world where customer service is disappearing, this Jersey City Mavis is proof that it still exists.

If you’re looking for a place that treats you like a person, explains what’s happening with your car, and stands on honesty even when it’s harder this is that place.

Much respect to Junior, the manager, and the entire team.
Highly recommended

I wish I could give zero stars. We came in for a simple brake maintenance check and were told all four brake pads needed to be replaced. After waiting five hours, we were then informed that the shop never had the pads in stock and needed to get them from another location. A few minutes later, that changed again—they could only replace the front pads.

So after five hours, only two pads were replaced, with no heads-up about the delay, no communication about the missing parts, and no transparency throughout the process. Extremely unprofessional. We won’t be returning to this location and strongly advise others to avoid it as well.

A week ago my mother came in for a tire change. When she came back with our car it reeked of gasoline although we thought it was due to the environment we were in. When the car started making a peculiar noise a few days later she took it back and they told her that the person who changed our tires also tampered with the oil tank and forgot to put the tank cap back on. At that point we have been driving for days with oil spilling out of our tank.

My mother stayed at this establishment that day waiting for a resolution or reimbursement of some sort but there was nothing offered or done. While seeing other reviews of this establishment I noticed that help has been offered to those who complained, but I find it very interesting that no one bothered to help my mother while she was there IN PERSON. When she asked for the name of the person who worked on our car, they refused to give it to her. They lack professionalism and accountability, I would NOT trust them with my car EVER again and neither should anyone else if you don't want to waste your money or time.

The fact remains: Mavis failed to deliver a proper installation, and that’s the core issue. At this point, Mavis should be taking responsibility and making things right, not hiding behind meaningless “we appreciate your feedback” statements.

I visited the Mavis location in Jersey City on September 29, 2025, for vehicle service, and picked up my car on October 1, 2025. Upon driving, I immediately noticed that the car was not operating smoothly and was pulling to the sides while driving.

Shortly afterward, I went to an authorized Meineke Car Center for an oil change, where they informed me that the installation work previously completed by Mavis had been done improperly. I obtained both video evidence and documentation confirming this issue.

I returned to Mavis with this information, where the team—led by Gregory (Greg)—inspected the car but insisted that everything was fine and that no further action was needed. This response was disappointing, especially given that I had spent thousands of dollars on the repair.

As a female customer who doesn’t have deep mechanical knowledge, I trusted Mavis to provide honest and professional service. Instead, I felt dismissed and not taken seriously when raising legitimate safety concerns. Later, when I visited an official Mini Cooper dealership for another issue, their technicians confirmed the exact same installation problem identified by Meineke. They also provided documentation and video proof of the improper work.

When I returned to Mavis to request a refund due to the poor workmanship and safety concerns, I was again dismissed. Greg’s behavior was particularly unprofessional—he was unwelcoming, dismissive, and showed little regard for customer concerns or safety.

On a positive note, I want to recognize Richard, who consistently provides excellent customer service and professionalism. He is truly an asset to this location and deserves recognition for his efforts. Unfortunately, Greg’s conduct completely overshadowed that experience and ensured that I will not be returning to Mavis in the future.

I ordered new tires through good year and chose this location for them to put my tires on. The same day I left, my tire low pressure light came on and I did a U turn and took my car back to this place. This guy said it was missing some air and that everything should be good after that. Left and went on about my day, driving around and the tire low pressure light still came on after that, so again I took it right back. I was very concerned at this point because now it’s the second time in this day that this light is coming on, after they said everything was “good”. So when I took it back there, they did whatever, checked whatever, and gave me this excuse of “it’s the sensor” but in my head I know that’s not right. I have a Honda accord 2022, there’s no reason why my sensor would just be going off for no reason, but they said everything should be fine after they checked it and did a test drive. This day was December 12th. So days go by I’m driving my car, and the light comes back on again, but I checked my tire and saw that it was flat. I bring it back in the morning, and there was this man with glasses, the same man that worked on my car, and he just had this impression that I was disturbing his day with my concern. I told him again that the light is on but also that my passenger driver tire is leaking air. This man tried to imply that if it’s flat it’s pretty much my fault and that there could be a nail in it. Lol. I did not like the way he was talking me, it was overall the attitude around his words that was starting to now annoy me. He said that he would have to charge me on whatever they have to do. I understood what he was saying but I felt like I shouldn’t have to pay anything because again, I’ve never had ANY problems with my tires until I took my car to them. I just left the place because I was afraid that if I came out of pocket just for them to tell me the same thing “it’s the sensor”, and basically tell me there’s nothing wrong with my tire, my money would be going to waste and I felt like the problem wouldn’t be solved. I told a friend about my tire situation, and he said it sounds like your rim is broken. So my friend took my car to his shop and yes indeed it was because my rim was broken and that’s why the tire was leaking air. But not only was it the rim being broken, the mechanic noticed that my tire had been cut, and he had to cut another piece from another tire to put on my tire to make it secure. You wanna know how my tire ended up being cut? From the same man that originally put my tires on, telling me it’s just the sensor. Lol. It’s just so ironic how when I brought my car to mavis, this man is telling me “it’s the sensor” but when I bring my car to a different location, they tell me my rim is broken and my tire was cut from them putting my tires on. So now I have to get a brand new tire because that same tire is still leaking air. I’m writing this review so that people can be aware of the people working on their car. It’s the fact that I knew something was wrong and he tried to make me believe it’s my fault that the tire is leaking air or make me believe that nothing is wrong but come to find out it was his fault because he broke my rim and cut my tire. Maybe he didn’t know…just maybe. Or he did know and just gave me that “it’s the sensor” excuse so I could get out his face. I really don’t know, but what I do know is that I’m never bringing my car to this location ever again.

I have been having a really hard time finding a reliable and trustworthy auto shop in the area. Being a woman, I am always nervous that shops are trying to take advantage of me but that was not the case at all here! After a horrific experience at PepBoys in Jersey City, I found Mavis online and saw their great reviews so decided to make an appointment for an oil change. This is my new go-to for my car needs!!

Greg was welcoming the minute I stepped foot in the door for my oil change! He went through the expected pricing and also offered to do additional checks free of charge such as air conditioning, brakes, etc. They also told me expected wait time for each additional check on top of the oil change (I was out in under an hour).

Joseph took my car back immediately and after seeing that my last oil change from PepBoys had stripped the plug in my oil filter, he worked to get the plug out and replace it rather than have to replace my whole oil filter. He also offered to bring me back into the repair shop to show what he was going to do before doing it to make sure I was aligned.

Besides the oil change, Joseph and Greg suggested I replace my air filters and also brought to my attention that I might need to replace my rear shocks soon since they had not been replaced and my car has 70k miles.

Not once did I feel pressured to do any of the additional services outside of my oil change, but I appreciated that they offered them along with how much time it would take them so I could better gauge the rest of my day. If you are looking for a reliable auto shop that also values your time, this is your place!

Today is Sunday June 1st 2024. I had an appointment to change one of my tire at this location between the hours of 4pm -5pm.This is the location by the Hudson mall in Jersey City. I bought the tire through Amazon and was able to choose my appointment time. I left work at 3:30 pm instead of my normal schedule which is 6pm. I got there about 4:30 pm and was met by the manager at the front desk. He was extremely rude. He ignored me for a good five minutes, then he asked, How can I help you? I explained that I had an appointment between the hours of 4pm-5pm to get my tire changed. He said, I have one car left and after that I’m closing up. I made sure not to get upset, because I’ve learned that getting upset does not help in any way. I told him I’ve been getting notifications reminding me to be here at this time. He told me, I don’t know who texted you but I have one car left and I’m closing after that. I said ok, may I have my tire that was shipped here? He went to the back and brought it to me. Then I asked, what about my refund for the installation? He said, you gotta call Amazon for that. I rolled the tire out to the car and put it in the back of my Jeep Grand Cherokee and called Amazon. An Amazon representative told me that they canceled my installation appointment after they received the tire from Amazon. Nobody from Mavis contacted me and I still continued to get the notifications reminding me to show up today for my appointment. If you are thinking about this service, I would strongly advise against it. There were at least 4 employees that I saw that day and I was within my appointment time and he still refused to do the service. If I could give them zero stars I would. What happened to customer service?
